missing_signal_ids.note: MSR_v3_0.md frontmatter and §I declare 499/500 signals respectively. The file actually contains 495 unique signal blocks numbered SIG.MSR.001 through SIG.MSR.496 with SIG.MSR.207 absent. SIG.MSR.497, .498, .499 are not present in the file. These four IDs are listed for audit transparency; they cannot be bucketed because they have no source content. Recommend: minor MSR metadata drift to flag in M4-A close or defer to M5+ MSR expansion.

empty_domain_signals.note: These 15 signals have `domains_affected: []` in MSR v3.0 — i.e., MSR itself does not tag them with any life domain. They fall into three semantic groups: (a) yoga-absence signals that mark non-formation of classical yogas (KSY, Hamsa, Ruchaka, Malavya, Durudhura, Chandra-Mangala, Sarpa, Mahapurusha-for-Sun); (b) parivartana exchanges; (c) meta/statistics/red-team signals (signal-distribution stats, confidence distribution, gap registers, prior-session error correction). All are appropriately placed in the `general` bucket; do not fabricate a domain for them.

notes: Domain assignments derived deterministically from each signal's `domains_affected` field in MSR v3.0 by the extraction script /tmp/msr_extract_v2.py. MSR v3.0 uses ten domain tags: career, children, family, health, mind, parents, relationships, spirit, travel, wealth. Mapping to the brief's ten buckets is one-to-many on the family side and identity elsewhere: career→career; wealth→financial; relationships→relationship; family/parents/children→family (collapsed because the family bucket absorbs all kin-related signals per the brief's category list); mind→psychological; spirit→spiritual; travel→travel; health→health. Education is absent from MSR's domain ontology, so the education bucket is empty by structural design — education-relevant signals (5H Mercury Jupiter 4H education-house architecture) flow through the career or general buckets depending on MSR's choice for each signal. M4-A T1 event-match work for education events should rely on house-based filtering (HSE.4 / HSE.5 / Mercury / Jupiter / DSH.AD lord whose karaka is education) rather than this domain bucket. Multi-domain signals are placed in their first-listed bucket (primary). Each such signal also has a row in `multi_domain_notes` recording all secondary buckets so retrieval for a given event domain can pull from primary-of-bucket-X plus secondaries-mentioning-X. No domain values fabricated; signals where MSR declares `domains_affected: []` are placed in general per the brief's uncertain-default rule and itemized in `empty_domain_signals` for audit. AC.T4.4 PASS (all 10 buckets present); AC.T4.5 PASS (495 of 499 claimed signals assigned; the 4 missing are absent from the source file, not unread); AC.T4.6 PASS (no fabrication; uncertain → general).
